description.task: Implement a CycleDetector class that uses Floyd's Tortoise and Hare algorithm to detect if a linked list has a cycle, find the cycle start node, and measure the cycle length.
instructions: hasCycle is already implemented — use it as a model for the other methods
findCycleStart: run slow/fast until they meet; then set one pointer back to head; advance both one step at a time; return when they meet (that's the cycle start)
cycleLength: find the meeting point (run slow/fast until slow==fast); then count steps for slow to return to the same node
Cycle: 1->2->3->4->5->3. Meeting point by Floyd's algo, cycle start is node 3, cycle length = 3 (3->4->5->3)
findCycleStart returns the node reference, print .val
description.hint: findCycleStart: detect meeting point first (same as hasCycle but save meeting). Then Node ptr=head; while(ptr!=meeting){ptr=ptr.next;meeting=meeting.next;} return ptr. cycleLength: count steps from meeting back to meeting.
description.expectedOutput:
Has cycle: true
Cycle start: 3
Cycle length: 3
No cycle: false

description.task: Implement a MergeSortedLists class that merges two sorted singly linked lists into a single sorted list in-place (O(1) space), and also implements merge sort on a linked list.
instructions: mergeTwoSorted: while both l1 and l2 are not null, pick smaller val, attach to curr.next, advance that pointer and curr; after loop, attach remaining non-null list
mergeSort, getMiddle, buildList, and listStr are already provided
Do not modify mergeSort or getMiddle

description.task: Implement an Intersection class that finds the node at which two singly linked lists intersect. Use the two-pointer approach: advance each pointer to the other list's head when it reaches the end, meeting at the intersection in O(n+m) time.
instructions: While pA!=pB: advance each (pA=pA.next, pB=pB.next); when pA==null set pA=headB; when pB==null set pB=headA
When pA==pB, return pA (could be null if no intersection, meaning both reach null together)
Intersection is at the node with value 8
